title: Maternal metabolomic signatures for risk of preterm and early term birth in African American women

data_scope.platform: Thermo Scientific Q-Exactive HF, HILIC positive ESI + C18 negative ESI

core_conclusion: High-resolution untargeted metabolomics of maternal serum reveals distinct, phenotype-specific metabolic profiles differentiating spontaneous from medically indicated preterm and early term birth in African American women. Perturbations in amino acid pathways (arginine, proline, methionine, branched-chain amino acids) dominate spontaneous early birth while acylcarnitines and aldosterone characterize medically indicated birth. Thirteen metabolites were internally validated; a six-metabolite panel for spontaneous PTB achieved AUC = 0.77.
why_it_matters: Addresses a persistent racial disparity (Black women experience PTB at nearly double the rate of White women); provides phenotypic granularity separating spontaneous from medically indicated subtypes; delivers an actionable validated biomarker panel; captures exposome signals (xenobiotics) via untargeted metabolomics; data deposited in Metabolomics Workbench for replication.
why_nature: Health equity focus on under-represented high-risk population; multi-dimensional mechanistic insight at pathway level; rigorous two-stage design (discovery + internal validation) with extensive sensitivity analyses; direct translational potential for prospective biomarker validation and clinical screening.
added_value_to_my_research: Geocode participants and link residential addresses to prenatal care access (R2SFCA) and environmental stressors — run spatially stratified MWAS to test whether metabolic PTB signals are spatially non-stationary
Integrate structural racism exposure surfaces (redlining, environmental justice burden) as MWAS effect modifiers to decompose disparity mechanisms via spatiotemporal kernel density estimation
Augment metabolite features with environmental exposome data and apply XGBoost + SHAP for explainable, per-patient metabolic risk attribution — bridging GeoAI methods into precision maternal health
Leverage computational mass spectral prediction to annotate the unannotated MWAS feature long tail — a data-fusion analogy to multi-source geospatial integration for FAIR/open-science work
Link validated metabolic PTB risk scores to residential PM2.5 surfaces via spatial mediation analysis to connect environmental justice geography to perinatal metabolomics

figures.caption: Metabolic pathway enrichment bubble plots for early (8-14 weeks) and later (24-30 weeks) pregnancy across six early birth phenotypes in HILIC and C18 columns
Box plots of serum concentrations or normalized intensities for confirmed metabolites significantly associated with sPTB, miPTB, sETB, and miETB vs. healthy full-term birth
ROC curves for six sensitive metabolic signals for spontaneous preterm birth across three phenotype models; best individual AUC = 0.74, multi-metabolite panel AUC = 0.77
